item.title: Things I send to my gf
item.description: How I'm going into the office on a Monday
item.dc:creator: MagillaGorillaTheStoneColdKilla

item.title: Every morning and night I send my two adult (22 & 20) sons, their mom, and their SO’s memes as a love note. I send dad jokes/puns, morning jams to guide their day, bizarre memes, words of affirmation, & nightmare fuel. Upvote if you enjoy. Love you all!
item.description:
Be silly today! I love you.
Get up! Wake up! Morning jams!
Eat your veggies or they’ll kick your ass.
I love you. Have a good day, boyos.
Birb is broke. Just like you.
Don’t sass me or I’ll show up to the next function wearing 1979 attire.
Happy birthday ? Sweet dreams. You’ll always remember THIS birthday!
Thank you, bathtub barracuda.
Aww, she looks just like our recently departed Jenny! We miss our doggo.
Sweet dreams!
Get up! Attack the day! Morning jams. May this beat be your rhythm throughout class and work today. It won’t drive you crazy at all! Best after a big cup of coffee or four.
These little fudgers always seem to be mad about something.
Get up! May this be your theme song every time you enter a room today! You’re amazing. I love you.
Cats are the devil’s toe jam. This has nothing to do with my allergies. #Honest
Get yo butt up! Attack the day! May this be your theme song for every obstacle. I believe in you. Love you!
I may start a business like this unless someone stops me.
Aww, Dustin (family downstairs Roomba) COULD take care of little freaks like this.
SCIENCE!
Ah, life in Baltimore and inaccurate stereotypes. Trust people you meet!
I love you, family.
April Fool’s!
Go ahead. Keep wearing black all the time, doofenschmirtzes.
To the maxilotl. Also, my autocorrect is jacked up. I wonder why?
Eh? Eh? Anyone?
I love you! I’m proud of you. Behave today. Or not.
Have a positive outlook even if the Eye of Sauron is upon you!
Kids these days.
Maybe this is why the crawfish is mad?
Perfect matchup. Love your SO!
This is for the T Swift fans out there. And the people who work with tools daily. (Meaning: all of you)
LOL
Birb! Here’s your study music today.
Lime is my favorite.
Get it? Ha ha! Dad joke, hoo ha ha.
Eat your damn food.
This will give you a buzz and the farts.
Dad advice.
I like that you exist. I love you.
Did you enjoy this Superb Owl?
Bad@ss birb
Is this why hibachi is your favorite?
I’ll keep trying to be silly. I’ll up my game.
I love you. Have a good night. Talk to you tomorrow. Love, Dad.
item.dc:creator: Wheretheheckisjason

item.title: All of the world’s billionaires younger than 30 inherited their wealth, research has found.
item.description: The research by Forbes magazine found there were 15 billionaires aged 30 or under but that none had created their own wealth, instead benefitting from huge inheritances. Among them are the three children of Leonardo Del Vecchio, the founder of the luxury sunglasses company Luxottica. They became billionaires after his death in 2022. The 28-year-old Leonardo Maria (pictured above with his wife Jessica Serfaty Michel) and his two younger siblings each inherited a 12.5% stake in the family’s Luxembourg-based holding company Delfin, which owns nearly a third of the company behind the brands Ray-Ban and Oakley. The siblings have a fortune estimated to be worth $4.7bn each. The world’s youngest billionaire is Livia Voigt, 19, who has a $1.1bn fortune thanks to a 3.1% stake in WEG Industries, a Brazilian electrical equipment producer co-founded by her grandfather Werner Ricardo who died in 2016. https://www.theguardian.com/business/2024/apr/03/all-billionaires-under-30-have-inherited-their-wealth-research-finds

item.dc:creator: exploradoresciclos2018

item.title: Remember to favorite and forget this post for later
item.description: Credit to Justin Wright. Which science-backed technique would significantly benefit how well you learn? Original post (I've shortened this so you don't have to read too much) ----- It's key to understand how the brain works regarding retention of information. It thinks in two main ways: Focused Mode: • You zero in on tasks and problems. • The Pomodoro Technique uses this mode. Diffuse Mode: • Your brain connects dots when relaxed. • This is when "aha!" moments strike. • And it's why breaks matter so much. 1. Dual Coding: Use words and pictures together to remember better. 2. Spaced Repetition: Fight the Forgetting Curve. Review learnings at intervals to make the info stick. 3. Memory Palace: Use your brain's knack for places. Link info to spots in a mental map. (I have personally never been able to do this successfully) 4. Pomodoro Technique: Study in short bursts with breaks to stay sharp. 5. Feynman Technique: If you can explain the info simply, like you would to a child, you truly get it. (Thus is my favourite!) 6. Interleaving: More advanced. Use variety to boost learning and problem-solving. 7. 20-Hour Rule: Learn a new skill in 20 focused hours. 8. Deliberate Practice: The key to skill mastery.
item.dc:creator: Suspiciouspenguin

item.title: I just wanted to share something
item.description: So ok,.. 5 years ago I became a father for the first time. A single father actually because my ex has a severe case of borderline and went above and beyond to keep me out of his life using the very reasons that are actually applicable to herself. I was so hopeless and helpless because back then the laws in the Netherlands were different and for me to get legal recognition of being the parent I had to have permission from the mother, which she wouldn't give me. She even called on protective services and told them I was dangerous and without so much as investigating any of her words told me I wasn't allowed to come anywhere near her house and my child. (Which I never even did in the first place) Apparently no one wanted to believe my story because if you're a man you must have done something wrong right? Not even my friends and familie (whom my ex had driven a wedge between) believed me. back then I made a musical project about it and also did a post on imgur. I'm guessing the way I told my story back then was all wrong because it got downvoted to hell even though the Imgur community is normally really supportive. It made me feel like shit. Like I wasn't worthy of being a parent. However I met a beautiful woman that actually supported me and she told me I should fight for the rights of parenthood legally and even though it would cost an arm and a leg she'd fully support me. She was right. She stood by me all the way. We now have a beautiful daughter and a lovely home together. I went to court, child protective services among others got involved and actually investigated the situation. Their conclusion was that everything my ex said about me was the opposite. She was in fact a danger to my son's development and her home situation was unstable. This is somehow not enough to get full custody but I never wanted that. I got official recognition that I was this boy's biological father and the right govern and see him. It is still a shit show however, because my ex tries to control every aspect of his and through him my life and obstructs our bonding in every way she can. Two years ago I did a project together with a female friend of mine who came up with the idea because she knew so many fathers in the same situation. My relationship with my son is still kinda awkward. Today I wanted to share the music I made with you guys. I don't know... I don't wanna be famous... I just want my music to be heard. I don't want it to be just a scream into the void. Everyone should have the right to see their kids. An imgurian called beyondthevoid actually encouraged me to make this post. So thank you dude! Here is the link: Everything written and produced by me, with guest appearances of several musicians. https://open.spotify.com/album/3mm048plqdVR88EXjfOQyw?si=ZbXsj6gsQiyqF8cT4ZMpeg i hope you guys like it!
item.dc:creator: unannouncedguest

item.title: The Void Dumps Back - Episode 8
item.description:
Hope everyone is having a great Caturday!
Stardew Valley farmer has entered th chat...
Free the mouse!
I like slightly cool water. Definitely not cold. Ice is a huge no-go.
Used to drink the little things of cream when I was a kid.
Is it good, though?
It me.
NOOOOOOOOOOOOOO
I have been listening to a lot of podcasts, and oh man, this drives me nuts.
*hugs* Edit - wow guys, never made most viral before. Thanks! If I get to ask for something, um... Just be excellent to each other, please. *individual hugs to everyone that wants one*
